paper.title: 591 high velocity stars in the Galactic halo selected from LAMOST DR7 and Gaia DR2

extraction.summary: Extracted the 20 fastest HiVelSC candidates from the printed tables of the paper. The full sample has 591 candidates but only the top 20 are individually identifiable in the provided files; the remaining 571 are not extracted. The paper reports at least 43 candidates unbound under Watkins+2019 potential.

method_chain.summary: Gaia DR2 astrometric and photometric catalog (positions, proper motions, parallaxes, G/GBP/GRP magnitudes).
Cross-match LAMOST DR7 with Gaia DR2 using TOPCAT with 5 arcsec radius, producing LAMOST-Gaia catalog of over 10 million entries.
Select stars with positive parallaxes and fractional parallax error f_ϖ ≤ 0.2, yielding over 8.48 million low-f entries.
Adopt LASP radial velocities with zero-point correction of -5.4 km/s derived from cross-match with Huang et al. 2018 standard stars.
Heliocentric distance by direct inversion of Gaia DR2 parallax (d=1/ϖ).
Solar position and motion: R0=8.2 kpc, z0=25 pc, LSR circular velocity 238 km/s, solar peculiar motion (U,V,W)=(14.0,12.24,7.25) km/s.
Adopt seven Galactic potential models: Paczynski+1990, Gnedin+2005, Xue+2008, Koposov+2010, Kenyon+2014, MWPotential2014, Watkins+2019.
Monte Carlo (1000 realizations) sampling of Gaia astrometry with covariance and LAMOST RV to compute Galactocentric positions (x,y,z), velocities (Vx,Vy,Vz), and total velocity V_GC.

source_refs.context: MC calculation of Galactocentric distance and velocity

method_chain.summary: Compute escape velocities at each star's position under the seven potentials and define unbound probability Pub as the fraction of MC realizations with V_GC > v_esc.
Orbit integration back 10 Gyr using Galpy with MWPotential2014, 1000 MC realizations per star; derive e, Zmax, r_min, E-Φ(∞).
Select candidates escaping under any potential or with V_GC ≥ 450 km/s, then visually inspect spectra and keep highest S/N for repeats → 591 HiVelSCs.
Compute Pgc (r_min < 1 kpc) and PMW (r_min < 25 kpc). Classify into HVS, HRS, RS, OUT using Table 4 criteria based on Pgc, PMW, Pub.
Estimate atmospheric parameters (Teff, log g, [Fe/H]) from LASP and data-driven Payne (DD-Payne), including [α/Fe] from DD-Payne.

source_refs.context: Atmospheric parameters from LASP and DD-Payne

method_chain.summary: Apply astrometric quality flag (astrometric_flag=1) and additional strict cuts (f_ϖ ≤ 0.1, σ_VGC/V_GC ≤ 0.2) yielding 92 conservative HiVelSCs.
LAMOST DR7 low-resolution spectra (10,608,416 spectra) with radial velocities from 1D pipeline and later LASP, plus derived atmospheric parameters.
Gaia DR2 astrometric parameters (RA, Dec, parallax, proper motions) used for phase space coordinates.
Gaia DR2 photometric bands (G, G_BP, G_RP magnitudes).

inclusion_assessment.summary: Hivel1 is the fastest HiVelSC in the sample with V_GC=922 km/s, and unbound probabilities Pub≥0.996 under all potential models. It is classified as HRS (hyper-runaway star).
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Values taken directly from printed tables and ECSV excerpts with clear provenance.

source_refs.context: Abstract stating 591 HiVelSCs unbound with Pub>50% under Watkins+2019

inclusion_assessment.summary: Hivel17 is one of the 20 fastest HiVelSCs selected from over 10 million LAMOST DR7 and Gaia DR2 spectra. It is classified as a hyper-runaway star (HRS) candidate with Pub > 0.5 under all seven potential models, and the paper reports it among the at least 43 candidates unbound to the Galaxy under Watkins+2019 with Pub ≥ 50%.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: All data extracted from printed paper tables (Tab:basic, tab:kinematic, tab:vesc, tab:unbound, tab:atmospheric) and verified against ECSV cells; clear candidate identification and consistent measurements.

inclusion_assessment.summary: Hivel19 is one of the 20 fastest HiVelSCs selected from over 10 million LAMOST DR7 and Gaia DR2 spectra. It is classified as an OUT (fast halo star) candidate, but its unbound probability under Watkins+2019 is 0.736, so it is among the at least 43 HiVelSCs the paper reports as unbound with Pub ≥ 50%.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: All data extracted from printed paper tables (Tab:basic, tab:kinematic, tab:vesc, tab:unbound, tab:atmospheric) and verified against ECSV cells.

candidate_groups_considered.description: The full sample of 591 HiVelSCs selected in the paper, listed in an external CSV catalog (http://paperdata.china-vo.org/LYB/lamostdr7_gaiadr2_hvs_591.csv). Only the 20 fastest candidates are individually printed in the paper tables.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: Only the 20 fastest HiVelSCs are individually identifiable from the printed tables and ECSV extracts; the remaining 571 candidates are not available for extraction.

source_refs.context: Abstract mentioning 591 HiVelSCs and external catalog
Construction of catalog and listing only 20 fastest in printed tables
